name: White Turnip Soup (Potage Aux Navets Blancs)
recipeIngredient: 4 slices to 5 sl good-quality white Bread, i.e., French, peasant
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a medium pot.
2. Add the coarsely chopped turnips and onions to the melted butter.
3. Saute over medium heat for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until onions are translucent.
4. Bring 6 to 8 cups of water to a boil. The amount depends on how much soup you wish to make and its desired strength.
5. Pour the boiling water into the turnip/onion mixture in the pot.
6. Break the bread slices into pieces and add them to the pot.
7. Heat the soup until it reaches a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for about 15 minutes or until the turnips are tender when pierced with a fork.
8. Set the soup aside to cool completely.
9. When cooled sufficiently, process the soup in a food processor or blender, or press through a sieve using a wooden spoon.
10. Season with salt, if desired, and pepper.
11. Before serving, reheat the soup gently until warmed through (do not boil).
12. Add the beaten egg yolks mixed with cream to the reheated soup and stir constantly until the mixture is blended and creamy.
13. Check seasoning and garnish each bowl with parsley or chives before serving.
